title: Tranquil Toasted Ethiopian Black Pudding Gomen
description: A buttery and aerated Ethiopian dish featuring black pudding, perfect for a lazy Sunday morning, with a flambe twist and ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly ingredients, served in a tagine pot.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter in the tagine pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the chopped collard greens and cook until wilted, about 3 minutes.
Add the sliced black pudding and cook for 2 minutes on each side, until browned.
Add the berbere spice, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in the red wine and flambe the mixture, until the wine is almost completely reduced.
Stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter until melted and well combined.
Serve the gomen hot, straight from the tagine pot.
